introduction: In Madhya Pradesh, marriage registration in urban areas can be accomplished digitally via the e-Nagar Palika portal. General applications are administered by the MP e-District infrastructure or local Gram Panchayats.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: 15 to 30 working days standard processing time post application verification.
  • Application Fee: Base processing fees vary nominally between urban and rural centers (usually ₹100-₹200).
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Marriage Evidence, and Witnesses.
Proof of Identity & Age
  • Birth Certificate
  • Samagra ID
  • SSC exams marksheet
Proof of Address
  • Voter ID
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Domicile certificate
Evidence of Marriage & Affidavit
  • Notarized affidavit outlining marriage date
  • Joined photographs
  • Wedding cards
Witnesses
  • Details of three witnesses
  • Witness ID documentation
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via MP e-Nagar Palika / e-District

In Madhya Pradesh, urban residents can apply through the MP e-Nagar Palika portal, while others can use the MP e-District gateway for online marriage registration.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit e-Nagar Palika Portal (Urban Areas)
    Go to mpenagarpalika.gov.in. For rural areas, visit mpedistrict.gov.in.
  2. Navigate to Marriage Registration
    Under "Citizen Services", select "Marriage Registration". Select your city / Urban Local Body (ULB).
  3. Fill Application Details
    Enter: full names of husband and wife, parents' names, date and exact place of marriage, religion, and details of 3 witnesses. Provide the Samagra ID if available.
  4. Upload Scanned Documents
    Upload self-attested scanned copies (JPG/PDF): age proof (Birth Certificate/10th Marksheet), address proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID), wedding card, joint photos, and 3 witness ID proofs.
  5. Pay Online & Book Appointment
    Pay the registration fee (around ₹100–₹200) online. Download the Acknowledgement Receipt containing your application number.
  6. Attend Office for Verification
    Both spouses and all 3 witnesses must physically visit the Registrar/ULB office on the scheduled date with all original documents for verification.
Download Certificate: Once the Registrar approves, you can download the digitally signed certificate directly from the portal using your application number.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Gram Panchayat or Ward Office

For rural applicants or manual offline registration, you can apply directly at your local Gram Panchayat or Ward Office / Registrar of Marriages.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate the Correct Office
    Visit the Gram Panchayat (for villages) or the Zonal / Ward Office (for cities) in the jurisdiction where the marriage took place. You can also visit the nearest Lok Seva Kendra.
  2. Obtain Application Form
    Collect the official Marriage Registration Form from the office counter. It can also be downloaded from the MP e-District portal.
  3. Fill the Form & Affidavits
    Enter all required details clearly in Hindi or English. Both spouses must sign a joint affidavit (notarized) declaring their marital status and marriage details.
  4. Attach Self-Attested Copies
    Attach: Samagra ID, Aadhaar (address proof), 10th marksheet (age proof), 3 witness ID proofs, joint wedding photo, and wedding invitation card. Bring originals.
  5. Submit & Pay Fees
    Submit the application file at the counter and pay the prescribed fee in cash. Collect your receipt and application number.
  6. Verification & Certificate Collection
    The officer will verify original documents and witness identities on the spot. After approval, the certificate can be collected physically or downloaded from the portal.
Note: Registration within 30 days is advised to avoid late fees. Processing takes around 15-30 days. Contact the CM Helpline 181 for queries.
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

In-Person Verification

Physical verification of the submitted photocopies with original ID cards happens on site at the Registrar location.

Issuance

Following officer approvals, the certificate becomes digitized and printable from the e-Nagar or Lok Seva portal.
